steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content:
An equilateral triangle has $3$ equal sides, so divide the perimeter by $3$.
$$
\frac{858}{3}=286
$$

steps.title: Use the circumradius formula
steps.content:
The circle through the vertices is the circumcircle. For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$,
$$
R=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{3}
$$
Substitute $s=286$.
$$
R=\frac{286\sqrt{3}}{3}
$$

steps.title: Match the coefficient
steps.content: The radius is given as $$ w\sqrt{3} $$, so match the coefficient of $$ \sqrt{3} $$ $$ w=\frac{286}{3} $$ The answer is 286/3.
